title: fix(libpod): truncate long hostnames to correct maximum length
body:
Since #24675, we've been truncating hostnames derived from the container name to 254 characters. However, if a user were to create a container with a long name and not set a hostname, this would still fail.

Seemingly, the maximum length for hostnames is (and always(?) has been) 64, as made evident by these headers from various kernel versions:

- https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.16/source/include/uapi/linux/utsname.h#L15
- https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v4.20/source/include/uapi/linux/utsname.h#L15
- https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v3.19.8/source/include/uapi/linux/utsname.h#L14

I've confirmed this issue (and fix) on Linux 6.1, but happy to do so on a more recent kernel as well.

```release-note
Fixed a bug where containers with no explicit hostnames, and names that were longer than 64 characters, would not start.
```

comments.body: Seems like CI is generally okay, at least with regards to my changes, so undrafting.

the code looks fine BUT I have debate about two things that others will need to weigh in on as well:

1. Do we consider this a breaking change?  I'm kind of leaning to yes?
2. AFAIK, hostname length is dependent on the OS. I think POSIX and others consider it to be 256 and others 64.

@mheon @Luap99 wdyt?

I did a `podman run -t -i --rm --hostname <128+ characters> fedora ls /` and got an error from `crun`, so I don't think this is a breaking change - it never worked in the first place.

`podman run -t -i --rm --name <128+ character> fedora ls /` does work, because it doesn't go into `/etc/hostname` - we don't use container name for that, we use a truncated container ID. So we're just using the name for DNS hostname. So if this breaks this - name set, hostname unset - then it is a breaking change. I worry that we've ended up in a confusing place where hostname and DNS hostname behave in different ways.

> `podman run -t -i --rm --name <128+ character> fedora ls /` does work...

I suspect this wouldn't work if `ContainerNameAsHostName` was true.
